chunks.text: John Wawrzynek is a Professor and CS Division Chair at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 631 Soda Hall, phone 510-643-9434, email johnw@cs.berkeley.edu, and office hours by appointment only. Administrative support is provided by Janet Vela at j.vela@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is John Wawrzynek's position and contact details?
chunks.answer: Professor and CS Division Chair at UC Berkeley.

chunks.text: John Wawrzynek earned a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from SUNY Buffalo in 1977, an M.S. in EE from the University of Illinois, Urbana/Champaign in 1979, and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Caltech in 1987. Before joining UC Berkeley's EECS faculty in 1988, he worked as a consultant at Schlumberger Palo Alto Research. His early honors include the Rensselaer Engineering and Science Medal in 1975, NASA Certificate of Recognition in 1983, and Charles Lee Powell Fellowship in 1985.
chunks.question: What are John Wawrzynek's educational background and early career?
chunks.answer: B.S., M.S., Ph.D. in EE/CS, consultant at Schlumberger.

chunks.text: John Wawrzynek's research areas include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), and he is associated with the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC). His selected publications cover topics like reconfigurable computing, FPGA systems, and video compression, with works such as 'Global Data Plane Router on Click' (2015) and 'BEE2: A high-end reconfigurable computing system' (2005). A complete publication list is available on Google Scholar.
chunks.question: What are John Wawrzynek's research areas and key publications?
chunks.answer: Computer Architecture, reconfigurable computing, FPGA systems.

chunks.text: John Wawrzynek has received notable honors and awards, including the DAC's Most Influential Paper Award in 2025, NSF Presidential Young Investigator (PYI) in 1989, and the Charles Lee Powell Fellowship in 1985. These recognitions highlight his contributions to computer engineering and research.
chunks.question: What major awards has John Wawrzynek received?
chunks.answer: DAC Most Influential Paper, NSF PYI, Powell Fellowship.
